description.en: A towering Czech Christmas braid: a big four-strand plait topped with a three-strand braid and then a twist, egg-yolk-rich and studded with raisins and almonds. Baked for Christmas Eve and morning, its tiers are read as the swaddled Christ child.
description.de: Ein hoch aufgetürmter tschechischer Weihnachtszopf: ein großer Vierstrangzopf, darauf ein Dreier- und zuletzt ein gedrehter Zweierzopf, reich an Eigelb und gespickt mit Rosinen und Mandeln. Zu Heiligabend gebacken, gedeutet als das gewickelte Christkind.
description.es: Una trenza navideña checa de tres pisos: una gran trenza de cuatro tiras, encima otra de tres y por último una torcida de dos, rica en yema de huevo y salpicada de pasas y almendras. Se hornea en Nochebuena; sus pisos representan al Niño Jesús envuelto.
description.fr: Une brioche tressée tchèque à trois étages : une grande tresse à quatre brins, puis une à trois, puis une torsade à deux, riche en jaunes d'œuf et parsemée de raisins secs et d'amandes. Cuite pour Noël, ses étages évoquent l'Enfant Jésus emmailloté.

origin.region: Czech Republic, also baked in Slovakia
origin.note: Documented since the 14th century in a Benedictine monk's writing; the older regional name Houska still lingers among Czech emigrant communities. Each tier and strand traditionally carries its own symbolic meaning.

ingredients.note: sliced, sprinkled on top before baking

yield.shape: three-tiered braid: 4 strands over 3 over a twist

keeping.note: Wrapped loosely, the butter and egg keep it soft for four to five days; it also freezes well whole or pre-sliced.

method.title: Bloom the yeast
method.summary: Warm the milk, stir in the yeast and a little sugar, and let it stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine the flour, remaining sugar, salt and lemon zest, then add the melted butter, egg yolks and the yeast mixture and bring together into a dough.

method.title: Knead and add mix-ins
method.summary: Knead on medium speed for several minutes until the dough passes the windowpane test, then work in the raisins and chopped almonds.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Shape into a ball, cover, and let rise somewhere warm until roughly doubled, one and a half to two hours.

method.title: Divide and braid
method.summary: Divide the dough into nine pieces of decreasing size and roll each into a rope. Braid four into the base, three into a smaller middle layer, and twist the last two for the top; stack the layers and secure with a few skewers.

method.title: Second rise
method.summary: Cover loosely and let the stacked braid rise until nicely puffed, about 45 minutes, misting it lightly partway through so the surface doesn't dry out.

method.title: Egg wash and bake
method.summary: Brush all over with beaten egg, scatter sliced almonds on top, then bake starting hot and finishing at a lower heat, about 40 minutes total, tenting with foil once the top is dark enough.

method.title: Cool and slice
method.summary: Remove the skewers, cool the loaf completely on a rack, then dust with powdered sugar before slicing.

source.adaptation: Cross-checked against two other Czech sources: salt nudged up slightly; almonds added into the dough, not just on top, matching several traditional versions. yeastPct is instant-yeast-equivalent.
